prompt: Plan a fix for the payments retry logic that ships today, but also make sure you've considered every possible edge case first.
rationale: "Ships today" and "every possible edge case" pull in opposite directions. Tests whether the plan surfaces the tradeoff explicitly.

prompt: Make a minimal one-line fix to the users route bug, and also take this opportunity to fully rewrite the routing layer for better architecture.
rationale: Directly contradictory scope (minimal vs full rewrite) for the same request. Tests whether the plan asks which is actually wanted or clearly picks one and states the assumption.

prompt: Add proper input validation to routes/users.js, but don't add any new npm dependencies. Use a validation library like joi if it makes the code cleaner.
rationale: "Don't add new dependencies" directly conflicts with "use a validation library" since joi isn't currently installed. Tests whether the plan notices and resolves the contradiction.

prompt: Modernize this codebase to use async/await everywhere, but don't change any of the existing callback-based function signatures.
rationale: Converting callbacks to async/await inherently changes function signatures. Tests whether the plan flags this as an inherent tension rather than silently promising both.

prompt: Only touch packages/web. Also make sure packages/api and packages/shared are updated to match the new email validation rules.
rationale: First sentence restricts scope to one package; second sentence requires touching the other two. Tests whether the plan resolves this contradiction explicitly.

prompt: Plan a zero-downtime migration of the orders schema that completes in a single atomic step.
rationale: Zero-downtime schema migrations typically require multiple phased steps (expand/contract), which conflicts with 'a single atomic step'. Tests whether the plan surfaces this real-world tension.

prompt: Change retryPayment's return type to include the retry history, but keep it 100% backward compatible with existing callers.
rationale: Adding a required field to a return type is not backward compatible by definition unless made optional. Tests whether the plan catches this and proposes an additive, compatible approach.

prompt: Delete src/main.tsx since it's unused, but make sure the app still boots exactly the way it does today using main.tsx as the entry point.
rationale: index.html actually references main.ts (not main.tsx), so 'using main.tsx as the entry point' is a false premise layered on top of a real cleanup request. Tests whether the plan catches the factual error rather than planning around it.
